content.problemStatement:
Implement a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) queue using a singly linked list. The implemented queue should support the following operations: push, pop, peek, and isEmpty.



Implement the LinkedListQueue class:



void push(int x): Adds element x to the end of the queue.


int pop(): Removes and returns the front element of the queue.


int peek(): Returns the front element of the queue without removing it.


boolean isEmpty(): Returns true if the queue is empty, false otherwise.
constraints: 1 <= number of elements in the queue <= 1000
1 <= value of elements in the queue <= 10^9
content.inputFormat: The input will contain a list of commands and a list of inputs. The commands will be one of the following: "LinkedListQueue", "push", "pop", "peek", or "isEmpty". The inputs will be a list of integers.
content.outputFormat: The output will be a list of results for each command. For "push" and "LinkedListQueue" commands, the output will be "null". For "pop", "peek", and "isEmpty" commands, the output will be the result of the operation.

solution.approach: Implement a queue using a linked list with the given operations.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) for push, pop, peek, and isEmpty operations
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is the number of elements in the queue
